title: Lithospermum callosum
intro: Lithospermum callosum, also known as Glaucium, is a flowering plant belonging to the borage family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, gastrointestinal issues, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Lithospermum callosum include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Lithospermum callosum is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
taxonomy: Lithospermum callosum is a species within the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is native to the Iberian Peninsula and North Africa. The classification of Lithospermum callosum is as follows: it belongs to the genus Lithospermum and the species L. callosum, and its full classification is Lithospermum callosum within the kingdom Plantae. The species is further divided into different sections and subsections within the Boraginaceae family. More specific classification details can be found in botanical databases and scientific research.

The plant's roots and leaves are used in some cultures to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions.
